summary: Great 1993 Deer Creek show with stellar Help‑on‑the‑Way/Slipknot! sequence and superb recording via the AMS audience mic.
review: Both reviewers rave about the tight playing and high energy of this three‑night run, highlighting a flawless Help > Slipknot > Franklin’s Tower stretch, gorgeous Looks Like Rain, and an expansive He's Gone. The AMS audience microphone delivers crystal‑clear stereo imaging with upfront crowd presence, earning a solid 4‑star sound quality rating despite a slight dip after the drums come in.

ai_rating.rationale: Two detailed 4‑star reviews consistently praise the performance and recording, with minor mixed comments that keep the overall rating just above four.

recording_quality.technical_notes: AMS audience mic captures excellent stereo image and upfront crowd noise; sound quality rated 4/5, pre‑drums clearer than post‑drums.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set builds tension with a killer Help‑on‑the‑Way/Slipknot! groove, second set rolls through jam‑heavy highlights (Looks Like Rain, He's Gone) before tapering off slightly after the drums re‑enter.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Shows an all‑out guitar showcase—fingerpicking on Help, shredding on Slipknot!, sweet solo on Looks Like Rain and dominant presence on He's Gone; a minor misstep on the final verse of Days Between.
band_member_comments.Phil: Confident vocal contributions (All Over Now) that lift the band's energy.
band_member_comments.Bob: Vince delivers barrel‑house piano licks and playful “falling down the stairs” solo; Bobby adds strong vocal theatrics but drops the ball briefly on All Over Now and goes over‑the‑top on Johnny B. Goode.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith (Vince) provides notable piano moments, especially before Franklin's Tower.
band_member_comments.Drums: Solid groove early in the show; reviewers note a weaker post‑drum segment.
